key_points: Gender division refers to unequal roles given to men and women in society
Patriarchy is a system where men dominate social and political life
Feminist movements fight for equal rights for women
Communalism refers to using religion in politics
Secularism means the state treats all religions equally
examples: Women performing most household work is an example of gender division
Demand for equal political representation for women shows feminist movement
Political leaders appealing to voters based on religion is communal politics
India allowing people to practice any religion is an example of secularism
formulas: Gender equality = Equal rights + Equal opportunities for men and women
Secular state = Equal treatment of all religions
exam_tips: Remember key terms like patriarchy, secularism and communalism
Use real-life examples when explaining gender inequality
Mention the role of the Indian Constitution when explaining secularism
Write short clear definitions for 2-mark questions
